descriptionParagraphs: The Flying Hippie is the next performance evolution of our retro-inspired models. For a change of pace from the shortboard grind, our team has been pushing the limits of our Bro Fish and Disco models from knee-high slop to overhead barrels. As one of our test pilots has gotten older, Kellen Ellison's shortboard days are more spread out, but his addiction to putting a board on rail or above the lip hasn't gone away. Designed with the Roberts signature blend of fun, performance, and versatility, the Flying Hippie pushes the retro vibe as close to modern performance as possible.

Built to easily generate speed, it has a parallel shape and a single-to-double concave bottom like the previous models. Volume has been maintained under your chest for paddling, while areas like the nose and tail have been thinned out to add responsiveness. The nose has been pulled in, and its rocker lifted slightly to surf tighter in the pocket and handle steeper waves and sections.
Category: ALL-AROUND ALTS.
Rocker: Low entry to flat throughout for easy wave catching and speed
Rails: Medium slight down rail with hard tucked edges for sensitivity and hold
Bottom: Single to Deep Double to Vee to create lift for speed while adding control
Fins: Standard as 5 fin to ride as twin, thruster or quad, can also be ordered with Twonzer placement
Sizing:

Compared to your good wave board go 2 - 4 inches shorter than your height for everyday surfing matching your volume at about 1.0 L more. At or over your height for a more cruisy feel with even easier wave catching and more glide. Comparing to pro model shortboards, ride it 5-7 inches shorter, 1" wider and slightly thicker (about 1.0 L extra volume). Custom dims are always available to match your needs or preferences. Please feel free to use our BOARD SIZING QUESTIONNAIRE to receive more info about our boards and sizing.

descriptionParagraphs: This board is not soft and slow! It's Fast and Floaty! A lot of softboards you see these days are great for getting to your feet but anything after that can be extremely frustrating. Long or short, their poor design with little detail holds back progress and performance. So what do you do if you're wanting to improve on a shorter board but still catch surf just as easily? Or what do you do if your grovel game is a beater and you want a small wave battleship that's a bigger blast to ride?

The Floaty has a full volume wide outline from nose to tail to give it that easy paddle stable feel. Low rockers provide natural speed while a little bit of flip in the nose helps to keep it from digging in on drops. The rails have been tuned for smooth surfing in mind while the outline can perform without hanging up. We do this by shaping a soft forgiving rail in the nose that graduates to a harder more performance rail in the tail of the board. The Floaty's rails are also tapered to let lighter riders turn with confidence and advanced surfers really push it to its limits.
Category: GROVELERS.
Rocker: Low rockers aid in paddling and speed
Rails: Soft entry rails for forgiveness transition to hard rails in the tail, overall tapered rails for easier turning and sensitivity
Bottom: Single to double concave to vee out the tail
Fins: Five fin is standard, works wonderful as twin, twin plus trailer, thruster or a quad
Sizing:

Determining the best length is be based on your desire to push yourself. Most improving novice surfers will be able to ride this board at their height to two inches taller for easy riding. Young groms, older or bigger surfers may need to go a little longer. Even total beginners can learn on the Floaty if they want to put themselves on a faster track to shortboard surfing. Lengths over 6' are available through custom order. For rail to rail performance groveling go 6 inches shorter than your pro model shortboard or 4-6 inches below your height and target around 3-5L more in volume. For cruiser groveling go about 10 L more volume than your normal good wave board. Custom dims are always available to match your needs or preferences. Please feel free to use our BOARD SIZING QUESTIONNAIRE to receive more info about our boards and sizing.
Related models: DREAM MACHINE · MUSH MACHINE

descriptionParagraphs: The Disco Hippy represents a progressive evolution that traces back to our retro-inspired yet performance-oriented Bro Fish. After wanting more performance in the next model, we introduced the Disco Diamond which retained that nostalgic vibe with some modifications such as a diamond tail for a shorter rail line as well as slightly thinner rails for added sensitivity. The result was a board that felt more responsive, easier to turn, and exceptionally agile in the pocket. Now with the introduction of the Disco Hippy, we have once again elevated performance while preserving all the wave-catching prowess and sheer speed of its predecessors.

Inheriting the flow, glide, and stability from the fish-type outlines, the Disco Hippy also keeps the rockers and concaves from its ancestors, maintaining impressive speed and release from the tail. The wide nose is still great for paddling while removing the beak reduces swing weight and improves maneuverability. Tuned thinner rails combine harmoniously to provide precise feedback and unmatched responsiveness in the pocket. A hipped squash tail introduces a break point in the outline, resulting in tighter turns and added hold during maneuvers. All together we've enhanced the Disco Hippy making it great for small waves as well as a way to progress or push your performance limits in a shorter board.
Category: ALL-AROUND ALTS.
Rocker: Low entry to flat throughout for easy wave catching and speed
Rails: Medium slight down rail with hard tucked edges for sensitivity and hold
Bottom: Single to Deep Double to Vee to create lift for speed while adding control
Fins: Standard as 5 fin to ride as twin, thruster or quad, can also be ordered with the Disco Diamond's Twonzer placement
Sizing:

Compared to the Disco Diamond you'll ride the Disco Hippy in the same length with a little less volume for easier maneuvering and performance. descriptionParagraphs: A transformation, the hybrid groveller. Out of its cocoon, our Metamorph takes the small wave groveller to another form. We wanted a board that can generate speed in small waves and control speed in waves where other grovellers might lose their hold. Inspiration came in the form of one of our most popular small wave boards, the Mutant. The Mutant crept its way into being a primary board for many and they took it to the limit. Like its predecessor, the Metamorph has low rocker forward of the tail area for speed with flip in the nose that doesn't hang up coming out of turns. In the rear, we opted for a thinned and tapered round tail with a hint of rocker. This gives it less surface area in the tail and a longer rail line to control speed going into tight turns or on better waves than what you'd find on just a grovel day. The bottom contours of the Metamorph start out as a single concave that blends into an extreme double concave that exits out the tail for lift and aiding the control of higher speed turns. If you want a board that thrives on speed and lives to destroy, discover the Metamorph.

descriptionParagraphs: On African grasslands jackals use their high sense of smell and their cunning nature to hunt for food. Instead of going with the more flowy feel of a wide retro swallow tail, the Jackal's tail is designed with an appetite for performance. From the mid point back, we blend the nose into a thinned winged swallowtail similar to the one found on our VLAD model. This type of tail gives it sensitivity and ease of performance at any speed. Working with the outline and foil, the rockers on the Jackal aren't as flat as found on grovelers nor as extreme as what you'd see on narrow shortboards. The nose rocker and width works well with front footed surfing to generate speed. descriptionParagraphs: The BioDiesel adds to our diesel class of boards a design that's part race-ready performance and part efficient daily driver. We started with our Turbo Diesel's rocker, outline, & foil and tuned it to create a high performance board that excels in average middle of the road surf. The BioDiesel is reactive in the pocket with another gear for creating and maintaining speed. First we looked at the Turbo's rocker and knew that we liked its ability for catching waves, accelerating off the bottom and arcing off the top. So we kept its rocker profile and then lowered both ends on the BioDiesel to make it an even better paddler and have more drive in waves that aren't that very steep or fast. We then widened the Turbo's outline from the middle through the tail so that the BioDiesel generates and maintains speed more easily with some added in glide for flatter faced waves. One final feature we considered was the Turbo's racy tapered medium rails. By carrying those over to the BioDiesel, it has that performance feel and easily gets on rail to take advantage of all its speed.

descriptionParagraphs: What makes it The Goods? If you're a shortboarder you're always looking for a board that can span a range of conditions and provide a high level of performance that isn't too far out of reach. And if you're new to shortboards you want a board that will give you confidence while not being too difficult to ride. A combination of the best features from some of our most popular and proven designs, The Goods is one of the most versatile boards we have in our lineup. Its moderate nose width and rocker maintains paddling while keeping swing weight down for sharp vertical turns. The Goods has an outline that leans more towards a subtle glide for smaller waves. while still being narrow enough for vertical surfing in waves with a little juice. The tail rocker is kept fairly low for speed and glide which helps immensely when dealing with slower sections found in softer waves or needing to create speed immediately in waves with tight pockets and a bit of punch. If you're looking for a board to take your performance in small to mid sized surf to another level, check out The Goods!

descriptionParagraphs: The Vindicator maintains our El Tormentor's advantages in small waves while a few design adjustments give it more performance and control in bigger surf. Super high performance thinned out shortboards are made to work best in perfect waves. The Vindicator is more for everyday surfing and even some better days you might luck into. For great paddling and gliding though flat spots, especially when it's small, it has a good amount of foam under your chest and low entry rocker. We knew low tail rocker like on the Tormentor works in a broad range of surf but its advantages are felt mostly on smaller or flatter faced waves. Wanting to give The Vindicator more upper range, we designed it with a more moderate tail rocker that still works terrific in small surf. Where it shines though is allowing you to control speed plus have the ability to pivot easier in the pocket on those days you're surprised the waves are better than your normal waist high go outs. Giving The Vindicator a rounded tail lengthens rail line and reduces tail area which is awesome for doing arcing turns on bigger faces while still having release off the top for vertical maneuvers in small to bigger surf. We also thinned its tail letting it be pushed around with less effort and giving it more sensitivity at higher speeds. Bringing it all together, The Vindicator is a really versatile as well as responsive high performance board. Or sized bigger, it can be a fantastic all around board for someone looking to get into shortboards or wanting more paddle.

descriptionParagraphs: The VLAD is a small wave destroyer. We created it with a fuller curvy outline than what you find on comp style shortboards so that it catches waves easily and glides in lack luster surf. We keep some of that width through to around the last 12" of the board for extra help in creating and maintaining speed in slower sections. As the tail starts to narrow, we reduce the tail area down with a winged swallow to give it sensitivity and ease of performance at any speed. Those wings work in conjunction with the swallow tail by creating pivot points and bite for tight turns and controlling speed when needed or if pushing the board into bigger steeper waves. White Diamond style rockers make the VLAD scary fast and adds to its wave catching powers. Some flip is added to the nose for making steep drops and attacking vertical sections. And medium rails shaped with a hard tucked edge in the tail area give the VLAD a performance feel when generating speed and flying through turns. Designed for speed like the rest of the board, the bottom contours add lift plus predictability. For anyone that dares, the VLAD rises to strike at will and rip through waves, none are to be spared.
Category: BETWEENERS.

descriptionParagraphs: With width forward of center linked with rockers and foils from our high performance shortboards, the Black Cobra paddles and handles extremely well. Since the first Dreamcatcher, our guys have been pushing the DC to its limits around California, Hawaii and Mexico. The DC's flatter rockers for easy planning and faster speeds is great in everyday waves and getting down the line in bigger surf. As the waves get bigger and you're moving faster, boards with flatter rockers tend to have less control. To take the design to another level, the Black Cobra was made to get in early like the DC and given additional rocker for more control and easier turning in the pocket at the higher speeds of bigger surf. Then back home, some of our riders started surfing on it more everyday than just using it as a step-up. Even though they love the performance of the DC, their style of surfing liked the addition of more rocker in the Black Cobra for its even tighter turning radius.

descriptionParagraphs: The T-33 was developed with Torrey Meister for his powerful progressive style. As his high performance board for surf trips and contests around the world, he wanted a board that would give him speed, instantaneous response and handle some juice when it presented itself wherever he might be. When we received the invitation to Stab in The Dark, we were asked to build the mystery surfer a board for the hollow beach and point breaks of Central America and Mexico. The T-33 was the first board that came to mind considering it was purposely designed for that type of surfer and those types of conditions. Besides knowing we'd be dealing with another of the world's best surfers, they also let us know he'd be 5'10 and 165lbs and the board should be a 5'10 1/2". Designing like we do for most customs but with a lot less information, the board's final dims were decided on and came out as 5'10 1/2 x 18 3/4 x 2 5/16 x 26.86L. Since the wave forecast and the timing of the trip didn't work out, it was decided that the mystery rider, who turned out to be Mick Fanning, would chase typhoon swell in Japan. And even though he didn't get to test it in ideal conditions, Mick raved on how fast and responsive the T-33 was even in the less than perfect stormy swell. Designed for maneuverability and flow in steep faces and tubing sections, the T-33 has a continuous rocker with a slight flip in the nose and tail. The rockers help the board fit the curve of steeper waves and provide for a tight turning radius at high speeds. Tapered rails and a domey deck work in conjunction with the T-33's rockers to add to its sensitivity for spontaneous moves in the pocket. For the all out speed the single concave gives it, a pulled in tail aids in control. In custom, a squash, swallow, or thumb tail can be ordered to dial in its performance. The T-33 is made to tee off on your local break or go in a travel bag on the trip of your dreams.

Rails: Medium tapered rails, thinning at the nose and tail

descriptionParagraphs: The Cruzee is a high-performance grom model designed for Cruz Dinofa. Cruz's first real board was a White Diamond. The wider outline of the White Diamond makes it a stable platform for groms while they're learning to make the drop and get down the line. As a grom progresses to using the rails of their board to generate speed, moving to a narrower lighter board is the next natural step in their progression. They need a board that lets them take advantage of their lighter weight and agility while taking into consideration their strengths and weaknesses. The Cruzee has been ridden in a wide range of conditions around the world so it is very capable over reef as it is in normal beach break. The Cruzee rockers are kept fairly tame to give the board speed and still be nimble while the nose has some flip in the last few inches to help prevent pearling. The rails are thin to allow easy turning while the deck is kept fairly flat to aid in stability. The bottom contours are subtle with a light single concave leading into a double concave in the tail to help the board release while being controllable. The idea in mind behind the Cruzee design is not to just shrink a pro board down. We've created a board for groms to enhance their strengths while helping their surfing grow stronger.

descriptionParagraphs: The Redneck is the combination of a high performance outline with our proven small wave rockers. The name comes to us by 2019 Junior World Champ, Dimitri Poulous and his perpetually sunburned neck. Dimitri had been testing this model for awhile and so we had asked him to come up with something to call it. After a full day of waves and way too much sun, the first thing that popped into his head was Redneck. Dimitri's favorite small wave contest board had been our Star Chip because it has plenty of speed and glide. His coach, Mike Lamm, suggested we modify it for Dimitri to add even more performance. The main objective for the Redneck was to maximize turns in the pocket while maintaining speed going in and out of maneuvers. To get the desired result, we narrowed up the outline and then gave it a rounded squash tail while keeping the special small wave rockers. The Redneck generates speed easily, while the narrower outline allows it to be surfed rail to rail and vertically very quickly. A reduction in swing weight in the nose makes for turns that are faster and more whippy than on typical small wave boards. Narrower in the middle encourages speed generation from quicker top to bottom surfing. And finally the Redneck's rounded squash reduces the tail area some but not too much. We wanted to make it easy to push through radical direction changes but still help maintain speed through flat or slow sections of the wave.
Category: HIGH PERFORMANCE SHORTBOARDS.
Rocker: Low in the tail for speed and slightly flipped in the last 12 inches of the nose to allow vertical maneuvers in the pocket

Bottom: Single to double concave

descriptionParagraphs: The Turbo Charger is the high-powered step-up evolution of our Turbo Diesel model. While the TD is celebrated for its speed and dexterity in punchy surf, the Turbo Charger takes it to the next level with enhanced features, ensuring easy entry and increased hold when facing steep and critical waves. Keeping the relaxed entry rocker of the TD, we've added a touch of thickness and width in the nose on the Turbo Charger. This provides exceptional paddling power to help you get over the ledge in steep waves. Combined with the TD's accelerated tail rocker, a narrower rounded tail gives the Turbo Charger heightened control in challenging conditions. Whether you're planning a surf trip to a place like Hawaii, Indo, or mainland Mexico or simply seeking more confidence on the best days at your local break, the Turbo Charger is primed and ready.
Category: STEP UPS AND BIG WAVE GUNS.
Rocker: Relaxed entry with a healthy amount of rocker from center through the tail
Rails: Tapered in the nose to medium through center going to medium thin through the tail
Bottom: Single to double concave
Fins: Standard with a five fin setup to be ridden as a thruster or quad
Sizing:

For this size guide use the information as suggestions for fit and dimensions where Novice is to be considered not a new surfer but someone that is new to surfing bigger waves. As a normal step up begin sizing 1-4 inches longer than your Turbo Diesel or your high performance shortboard with thickness up ⅛". Go longer and thicker as wave height increases. Having said that, step-ups are best tuned to your personal needs and the waves you'll be surfing. Please feel free to use our BOARD SIZING QUESTIONNAIRE to receive more info and sizing for your next trip or for a board that can handle bigger waves in your area. To purchase our Step Ups or Big Wave Guns, use the questionnaire to begin the process or call the factory to speak with Robert directly at 805.658.6855

descriptionParagraphs: The Woolly Mammoth is our big wave paddle-in board with a proven rocker that we've been developing for over 20 years via personal experience and rider feedback. Created to be ridden in the biggest Hawaiian waves, the Woolly Mammoth has also helped riders conquer places like huge Mavericks, Puerto Escondido and Todos. Its volume is designed to be concentrated where needed for getting you in early and is combined with a narrower tail for controlling speed while moving down the face. Knowing the Woolly Mammoth is going to be placed in some trying conditions, we use blanks with a wide stringer, insert dual leash plugs and glassing consists of double 6oz cloth on top and 6oz cloth on the bottom.
Category: STEP UPS AND BIG WAVE GUNS.
Rocker: Slightly relaxed entry for paddling to a long continuous rocker in the middle for extra glide speed that gently accelerates out the tail for controlling speed in powerful waves.

Rails: Thick for paddling but tapered down to lower their apex, soft in the very front for forgiveness to a medium soft through the middle for hold and forgiveness going to hard edges in the tail for hold on high speed bottom turns.
Bottom: Slight vee in the nose to flat in the middle going to light double concave to vee out the tail.
Fins: 5 fin set up to be ridden as quad or thruster.
Sizing:

Custom orders are sized to your individual needs in lengths typically 9'0-11'0. Custom dims are always available to match your needs or preferences. descriptionParagraphs: Not all longboards are made to cruise. The HPLB is designed to create speed and attack waves with the same maneuvers you see from shortboarders. Continuous rocker running nose to tail makes it sensitive and quick in the pocket and ready to turn on rail out on the shoulder. To assist in the turning and provide hold, the HPLB has beveled rails in the nose with 40/60 rails through the middle to shortboard-like hard edges in the tail. The nose area has a slight bit of concave that runs through the board to the tail area which has double concave with vee set inside. The HPLB is available in stock or custom dims in lengths between 8' and 10'. Advanced level or lighter riders can order thinner (starting at 2 1/2" thk) for increased sensitivity. For another level of performance, we suggest our RET (stringered EPS/Epoxy) construction for reducing weight while adding strength.
Category: MODERN CLASSICS.
Rocker: Continuous rocker for cutbacks and off the lips, not too extreme so that some noseriding is still capable
Rails: Beveled rails in the nose with 40/60 rails through the middle to shortboard-like hard edges in the tail
Bottom: Slight concave in the nose that runs through the board to double concave with inset vee in the tail.
Fins: Box Plus Two to ride as a single with or without side bites for added hold; can also be ordered with a 5 fin setup to ride as thruster or quad.

The Classic LB has flat rocker in the front for surfing from the nose that blends into ample rocker in the tail to make turning easier. 50/50 rails that are tapered and soft give it just the right amount of sensitivity. Its bottom contour starts with a slight concave that goes into a rolled vee through the rest of the board. For additional noseriding capabilities, Robert suggests the Classic LB with optional scooped concave in the nose to help you control speed while riding forward on the board. Many other options are also available for customization and glassing such as airbrush, resin tint, Volan cloth and gloss finish.

descriptionParagraphs: The Modern Mid bridges the gap between mid-lengths and our high-performance Dreamcatcher. Its forward wide point and curvy outline provide the enhanced paddling and glide you get from a mid-length, while its other features unlock an elevated level of performance that those types of boards typically don't offer.

A slightly wider but thinner nose than the Dreamcatcher helps paddling and keeps swing weight down for agile performance and a lighter feel in turns. Tapered down rails with their thinner profile add sensitivity. Combined with rockers designed for a blend of speed and flow, allow the Modern Mid to go on rail and turn easily. A single to double concave bottom and rounded pintail from the DC provides lift as well as better control in a broad wave size range.

One of the most unique performance features we put into the Modern Mid is its fin box configuration. It has normally placed front fin boxes as well as a single fin box for riding the board as a single fin, single fin with side bites, or as a thruster. AND we install two extra boxes in a special placement to ride it as a twin fin or bonzer variant for greater speed with even more responsiveness.

When sized between a shortboard and a classic mid-length, the Modern Mid offers a level of flow and performance not typically seen in longer railed boards. In longer lengths and higher volumes, it suits lighter, newer, or older surfers seeking effortless paddling and stability. A fusion of function, performance, and style, you can choose your Modern Mid to perfectly fit your surfing, whether you want to carve up the wave or cruise on down the line.
Category: MODERN CLASSICS.
Rocker: Low to moderate rocker with a small amount of flip in the nose
Rails: Medium tapered rail with hard tucked edges for sensitivity and hold
Bottom: Single to double concave
Fins: 5 fin boxes for Single, Single with side bites, or thruster in the main boxes or use the specially placed boxes to ride as a twin or bonzer variant
Sizing:

Size it to match your preference, ability, and/or volume needs. The weights in the chart above approximate lengths for a performance feel with some flow built in going about 4" to 8" over your height. In more of a mid-length feel with additional glide, stability and easier paddling, go 8" to 12" over your height with 10" over as the sweet spot. Custom dims are always available to match your needs or preferences. Please feel free to use our Board Questionnaire form under the Q&A tab for more info about our boards and sizing.
Related models: Dream Maker · Dreamcatcher PLUS · Dreamcatcher · Invader

descriptionParagraphs: Everyone knows in our time as the waves get smaller the competition to catch a wave grows. Our Classic Mid Pin revives 1960s-inspired design to conquer the challenges of today's crowded lineups thanks to its well-distributed volume. With that, the Classic Mid Pin ensures exceptional paddling, performance, and stability while providing an ideal balance of timeless style and versatility.

The Classic Mid Pin brings back the essence of those early performance days when style reigned supreme and surfers were searching for more maneuverability. With its flatter rocker, similar to a longboard, you get unparalleled glide and speed. However, its shorter length combined with its pintail makes it easier to maneuver. The thinned and turned-down rails enable easy speed generation that work with the tail design for smooth rail-to-rail turns.

If you're looking for a board with effortless glide on mellow waves, the Classic Mid Pin is crafted to maintain the stylish performance of previous generations that still remains at the pinnacle of small wave surfing today.
Category: MODERN CLASSICS.
Rocker: Slight in the nose with increased rocker off the tail
Rails: Tapered down rails
Bottom: Forgiving entry to a light vee blending into a double concave from below the mid point through the fins, a light vee off the end of the tail
Fins: Single + 2
Sizing:

Size it to match your preference, ability, and/or volume needs. Keep in mind with more volume comes more glide and more drawn out turns, so we can adjust all of that in a custom if you prefer. A general rule would be go 8" over your height for easiest turning in this style of board to 14" over your height for more glide, stability and easier paddling. For a small wave version or wanting even more wave catching ability, go longer or order custom with added thickness. Custom dims are always available to match your needs or preferences. Please feel free to use our Board Questionnaire under the Q&A tab for more info about our boards and sizing.

descriptionParagraphs: The Invader is a stealthy wave destroyer. Beak nose, fuller volume, and mid-length describe features of boards from surfing's past but they also add function to a style of performance board that makes the Invader very modern. The beaked nose brings volume forward into its longer length for paddling around the lineup with ease and catching waves the waves you want. With a touch more rocker in the nose and tail than boards of past, the Invader makes steep drops and rebounds off white water much easier than retro crafts. Down rails lower the rail apex for a thinner feel while giving the Invader more hold and quicker turning ability.
Category: MODERN CLASSICS.
Rocker: Slight flip in the nose to moderate in the middle, ending with a very light flip in the tail
Rails: Low down rails throughout, soft tucked bevel in the entry going into a hard edge in the tail for hold
Bottom: Very light vee in the tip of the nose going flat in the first 8 inches then it goes in to a single concave then a double to light vee off the end of the tail
Fins: 5-Fin Twonzer is standard to ride as Twonzer with Roberts Twonzer fins or thruster, can also be ordered custom with other setups
Sizing:

The Invader is designed to be ridden above your height by at least 4 inches to be an easy paddling but still rippable mid length board. Looking for easier paddling size it longer than the chart might suggest for your weight to a comfortable length and volume. Custom dims are always available to match your needs or preferences. Please feel free to use our BOARD SIZING QUESTIONNAIRE to receive more info about our boards and sizing.

descriptionParagraphs: As surfers began to search for the barrel in the late 60s and 70s, longboards were being traded for shorter and narrower single fin boards. Flatter rockers made these smaller boards still great paddlers while their narrower outlines gave them more maneuverability in the pocket and out on the shoulder than their longboard predecessors. With these new boards taking over, the style of surfing moved away from walking to the nose to stylishly trimming down the line and looking for more progressive ways to use speed.

Our Classic Single + 2 brings back that flowing feeling of the 70s as a single fin with the ability to use two side fins depending on conditions or preference. By using two small side bite fins with your single, you'll feel more hold and gain more drive. With a smaller single fin and normal thruster sides, you'll get increased performance with a more controlled feel similar to modern boards. For a totally different trip, you might go with a set of twin fins like our upright keels for plenty of drive with a free flowing feel in turns.

For below average days to epic conditions, the Classic Single + 2 gives you a great paddling board with plenty of speed for the kind of fun that has drawn people to surfing since the start of the performance era. Its fuller outline helps give it glide when the waves are weak while its ample volume allows you easy and early entry when you need it. As an fun alternative to a rockered out step up or too big longboard when the waves are better it can be an asset in anyone's quiver from beginner to expert.
Category: MODERN CLASSICS.
Rocker: Low entry to flat throughout for easy wave catching and speed
Rails: Medium slight down rail with hard tucked edges for sensitivity and hold
Bottom: Flat bottom to rolled vee out the tail
Fins: Single + 2
Sizing:

Ideally ridden at least 4 inches above your height for an easy paddling mid length board. Looking for an even easier paddling mid length feel, size it bigger than the chart might suggest for your weight to a comfortable length and volume. Custom dims are always available to match your needs or preferences. Please feel free to use our BOARD SIZING QUESTIONNAIRE to receive more info about our boards and sizing.

descriptionParagraphs: While following in the path of its predecessor, the Twinvader brings another level of performance to the fray. It maintains the Invader's wave catching reputation with an even more agile feel. The Twinvader's narrower nose reduces swing weight which allows it to react faster and come around in turns more quickly than its more mid-length counterpart. Although the nose is narrower, the wide point has been moved out slightly to give it a bit more glide. This helps it carry speed down the line in smaller surf and flatter faced waves. The Twinvader's twin fin setup gives it a looser more pivoty feel. This is especially beneficial if wave speed is lacking like you might see in fat beachbreak or slow point surf.
Category: MODERN CLASSICS.
Rocker: Slight flip in the nose to moderate in the middle, ending with a very light flip in the tail
Rails: Low down rails throughout, soft tucked bevel in the entry going into a hard edge in the tail for hold
Bottom: Very light vee in the tip of the nose going flat in the first 8 inches then it goes in to a single concave then a double to light vee off the end of the tail
Fins: Twin or Twin with trailer
Sizing:

Ride the Twinvader 2-4 inches less than your Invader for a longer railed twin fin feel. This works out to be at your height or a couple inches over. You can go shorter for additional performance. Match volume to your desired amount of flow. If you want more paddle and control you can always size it up longer with more volume.Custom dims are always available. descriptionParagraphs: Back in the 60s, it was a time of experimentation and everyone was looking at the world differently. Steve Lis, a San Diego kneeboarder and forward thinker, shaped a board for himself in 1967 that would change the course of surfing. All it took was loaning it to one of his standup surfer friends who was on a different level like Steve. His friend kept on borrowing it because he was blown away by the maneuverability it had from its short length and the speed it got from its flat bottom and twin fins compared to the longer single fins of the day. Steve eventually got tired of loaning it out, so he shaped himself one and the rest is history. Just like what those dudes were searching for back in the day, our Classic Fish handles a wide variety of waves, paddles really well and turns easily. It's also a very forgiving and user friendly option compared to modern performance designs. By sharing characteristics from those early fishes made over 50 years ago, our Classic Fish has ample volume and stability for plenty of glide and speed. That speed and agility is just like what was found so long ago on those first rides down in San Diego. And like then, our Classic Fish will open your eyes to another dimension of surfing.
Category: MODERN CLASSICS.
Rocker: Low entry to flat throughout for easy wave catching and speed.
Rails: Medium slight down rail with hard tucked edges for sensitivity and hold.
Bottom: Flat bottom to rolled vee out the tail
Fins: Twin Fin, our Roberts Twin sets are a great option
Sizing:

3-5 inches shorter than your height for everyday surfing, matching your volume at about 2L-4L more than your good wave board. Over your height for a cruisier feel with even easier wave catching and more glide. Custom dims are always available to match your needs or preferences. Please feel free to use our BOARD SIZING QUESTIONNAIRE to receive more info about our boards and sizing.

descriptionParagraphs: First board, fun board or one board, The Fun 1 is a blend of easy surfing and performance. The Fun 1 is a board we've been making for many years and has been continually perfected over time. Some similar looking boards are like a flat rockered mini longboard which can make drops and turns down the line difficult. To counter act that The Fun 1's rockers start with a slight flip in the nose to a moderate rocker in the middle and ending with a very light flip in the tail. That bit of nose flip helps to prevent pearling and hang ups. Moving back from the nose there is some curve in the rocker but not too much as to take away from easily catching waves. But it is enough so The Fun 1 will turn smoothly and with more ease when the rails are applied. This is one thing that makes it so versatile for the beginner to the advanced. As your surfing improves you'll be able to take advantage of the rocker instead of a flat board holding you back. And the beyond beginner riders can jump on it right away and feel comfortable in all kinds of surf. Medium instead of full rails aid in its stability and turning. This helps lighter riders turn and more advanced riders turn sharper. On the bottom it has single concave through the entry to a double concave in the tail for lift, speed and control. It has a slightly pulled in squash tail. A full nose outline also helps for paddling. Depending on how you size it, The Fun 1 can be great for a beginner, someone looking for a longboard alternative or even the advanced surfer wanting to catch more waves.
Category: MODERN CLASSICS.
Rocker: Not a flat rocker found in typical 'funboards'. Slight flip in the nose to moderate in the middle ending with a very light flip in the tail
Rails: Medium.
Bottom: Single concave through the entry to a double concave in the tail.
Fins: Five fin is standard to ride as a thruster or quad, can also be ordered custom with a single fin box and side bites.
Sizing:

Size The Fun 1 for a beginner according to our size chart based on weight or a length that is comfortable. Looking for easier paddling size it longer than the chart might suggest for your weight to a comfortable length and volume. Advanced surfers size them many different ways. It can go shorter than your height while matching your volume needs slightly higher than your shortboard. Or above your height (even several inches) to have an easy paddling but still rippable mid length board. Custom dims are always available to match your needs or preferences. Please feel free to use our BOARD SIZING QUESTIONNAIRE to receive more info about our boards and sizing.
